purpose: Define a compact but expandable Galaxy Guardians game specification language so future gameplay adjustments are described as stage-band, role, movement, scoring, audio, visual, and promotion-rule changes instead of scattered tuning constants. This is also an early proving contract for a broader Platinum game-definition language that should eventually describe Aurora and future games.

summary.currentRead: Guardians now has enough score, movement, stage-band, and routeability evidence to benefit from a broader game-spec language before more constants are tuned.
summary.scope: This contract describes the current 0.1 preview and future adjustment proposals. It is not a claim that all public lanes expose the deeper stage-band runtime yet.
summary.primaryUse: Future Guardians changes should be proposed as spec deltas with affected stage band, role family, movement primitive, scoring/result effect, audio/visual hooks, and promotion gates.
summary.longTermUse: Platinum should increasingly execute games from this kind of specification language rather than from hard-coded, game-specific control paths, while preserving each game's lineage and evidence.

platformLanguageStrategy.direction: Guardians is the small proving ground for a specification-like game definition language that can expand over time from planning contract to platform execution contract.
platformLanguageStrategy.platformGoal: Platinum should eventually use the game specification language as the primary way to execute and validate games: pack identity, stage arcs, enemy roles, movement primitives, scoring, audio/visual hooks, result states, and promotion gates should be declarative enough for shared platform systems to run and inspect them.

crossGameScope.role: smallest active proving game for spec-first runtime changes
next migration target once Guardians proves the language can describe a real playable loop without losing lineage-specific behavior
future third-game test that the language is not only Galaxian/Galaga shaped

platformLanguageStrategy.ingestionGoal: The same language, or a highly related ingestion dialect, should be populated from video gameplay analysis: source manifests, frame windows, event logs, object tracks, audio windows, sprite crops, routeability reads, and correspondence scores should become candidate game-spec deltas.
expectedPlatformChanges: load and validate game spec artifacts as first-class pack contracts
map declarative role and stage-band definitions into runtime state machines
route scoring, replay, pilot records, and high-score ownership through game spec identity
bind movement primitives to reusable runtime executors where evidence supports promotion
let ingestion outputs propose spec deltas before runtime constants change
keep per-game escape hatches only where the shared language cannot yet express a real lineage requirement
nonGoals: forcing every game into the same behavior
promoting generated ingestion candidates without human/evidence gates
removing game-specific code before the language can execute the behavior safely

owns: result titles and one-level mission framing

bands.playerFeeling: Invitation, score literacy, cabinet identity, and single-shot expectation.
Readable rack, first dives, first loss, and fair single-shot learning.
Pressure rises without collapsing into unreadable collision stress.
The session is underway and mastery matters.
High-pressure arcade survival that is still the same game.

roles.playerMeaning: Common lower-value alien that teaches solo dive pressure and single-shot discipline.
Mid-value alien that makes flagship attacks feel coordinated and scoreable.
High-value command alien that creates special pressure and reward moments.
Single-shot player ship with clear missile readiness and vulnerable lower-field route choices.

specLayers.promotionRule: Movement changes cannot promote from object-track or pressure fit alone. They must preserve or improve routeability, collision safety, shot windows, visual readability, and player meaning.

specLayers.publicSliceRule: The current public slice may end at one-level mission_complete while deeper stage-band metrics remain internal conformance evidence.
specLayers.fullGameRule: A fuller arcade game should treat player loss after sustained pressure as the canonical full-run ending while preserving score, stage reached, and game identity.

visualHooks: mission complete and game over result
specLayers.rule: A gameplay adjustment must declare whether audio and visual hooks are unchanged, required, or intentionally deferred.
Spec deltas must name the affected layer and pass the smallest focused gate plus the aggregate first-class gate before being described as a quality improvement.
Platform changes may be required as the language moves from planning artifact to executable game definition. Until then, the spec remains an evidence-backed contract for safe implementation.

ingestionCandidateTemplate.rule: Video-derived ingestion should propose candidate spec deltas, not silently mutate runtime behavior.

nextSteps: Use this contract as the intake shape for the next lower-field readability and alien-ship pace candidate.
Promote the next candidate as a spec delta before changing runtime constants.
If the pattern works for Guardians, extract a shared game-spec schema that can cover Aurora challenge stages and Windigo.
Plan the first Platinum platform change that treats a game-spec artifact as a loaded, validated pack contract rather than only documentation.
